title: crambe maritima var. pinnatifida
intro: Crambe maritima var. pinnatifida, also known as sea kale, is a subspecies of the Crambe maritima plant that is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. This perennial plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant's leaves and stems can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions. Additionally, sea kale has been used as a food source in the past, and its leaves can be eaten raw or cooked, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
